name: General Settings (write)
description: The /settings/general page (routed via the settings-general override). Covers a settings WRITE flow as Unlock-2 effect:write transitions: the 'Auto-load Last Configuration' Radix switch (#auto-load) defaults to aria-checked=false. The forward write transition clicks it ON (waitAfter asserts aria-checked=true), and the paired revert transition clicks it back OFF (waitAfter asserts aria-checked=false, the page default). The toggle is purely client-side React state on this page — it persists to the runner only when the page's 'Save' button is clicked, which these transitions deliberately never do — so the write mutates NO shared server state and is trivially reversible. The structural state asserts only that the two switches exist; their values are behavioral (asserted via the transition waitAfter), not structural.

states.name: General settings switches
states.description: The two preference switches on /settings/general: 'Auto-load Last Configuration' (#auto-load) and 'Include AI Summary in New Workflows' (#include-summary). Asserts presence by DOM id (stable, author-set) — never the on/off value, which is behavioral.

assertions.description: Auto-load Last Configuration switch (#auto-load) is present.
Include AI Summary switch (#include-summary) is present.

transitions.name: Toggle Auto-load ON (write)
transitions.description: Behavioral WRITE: click the 'Auto-load Last Configuration' Radix switch (#auto-load, defaults aria-checked=false). The waitAfter IS the assertion: the same element now reports aria-checked=true (Radix reflects checked state on the DOM attribute, which the live-DOM matcher reads via getAttribute). effect:write — it flips client-side React state on this page; it does NOT touch the server (the page's Save button, the only persist path, is never clicked). activateStates is empty because the assertion is the attribute flip captured by waitAfter, not a separate structural state. Paired with the revert transition below so the page is left at its default (off).

transitions.name: Toggle Auto-load OFF (revert)
transitions.description: Behavioral REVERT (paired with the ON write above): click the same #auto-load switch again to restore its default. The waitAfter asserts aria-checked=false — i.e. the in-spec revert returned the control to baseline, so this write leaves no residue even within the shared ci-bot session. effect:write (still a mutation verb; reversible). Runs immediately after the ON transition on the same page render, so it operates on the live, already-toggled switch.
